title: Top 10 House Wires in India (2026): Best Value for Money
description: The 10 best-value house wires in India for 2026, ranked by the safety and quality you get per rupee. Compare Polycab Maxima+, APAR Anushakti, KEI Conflame, Havells & RR Kabel on Elume.
author: Elume Editorial
intro: House wiring is a once-in-thirty-years buy, so the goal isn't the cheapest coil, it's the best value for money: the most fire safety, heat resistance and copper quality you get for every rupee. This 2026 guide ranks the top 10 single-core copper house wires the way a smart contractor actually shops, weighing insulation grade and safety specs against the real per-metre price on Elume.

The grade ladder matters most. Plain FR only slows flame spread; FR-LSH adds low smoke and low halogen for enclosed spaces; HR FR-LSH adds heat resistance (85 C); e-beam cross-linked (EBXL) grades push to 105 C with the longest life; and HFFR is fully halogen-free for the strictest fire codes. Every wire below is single-core, 99.97% electrolytic-grade bare copper to IS 694, 1100V, in a 90 m coil, so the ranking comes down to grade, specs and price, not the basics.

items.name: APAR Anushakti (EBXL HR FR)
items.bestFor: Best overall value: premium safety at a near-standard price
items.body: APAR's flagship uses electron-beam (EBXL) cross-linked HR FR-PVC rated to 105 C, with a critical oxygen index of 30%+, plus claimed 50% higher current capacity and a 50-year life. You get genuine top-tier heat and fire performance at a per-metre price close to ordinary FR wire, and on Elume it lands around 46% below MRP. For most homes wanting the most protection per rupee, this is the pick.

items.name: Polycab Maxima+ Green Wire (HR FR-LSH)
items.bestFor: Best mainstream value: HR FR-LSH at an FR price
items.body: Polycab's Maxima+ Green Wire is HR FR-LSH, low-smoke low-halogen insulation to IS 694, 85 C rated, from India's largest wire maker. It is one of the most affordable premium wires per metre on Elume and carries the deepest discount here (around 54% off MRP), so you get low-smoke fire safety without paying a premium. Ideal for enclosed homes and commercial fit-outs.

items.name: KEI Conflame Green+ (HR FR-LSH)
items.body: Conflame Green+ is KEI's HR FR-LSH line: 85 C rated, oxygen index 31%+, and the highest 2.5 sq mm current rating in this list at 28A. Low smoke and low halogen keep enclosed spaces safe, and the heat headroom suits AC, geyser and kitchen runs. Strong value for a premium grade.

items.name: RR Kabel Superex (FR)
items.bestFor: Best budget value: lowest cost per metre
items.body: Superex is RR Kabel's FR house wire (LOI >29%, temperature index >250 C, IS 694). It is by far the most affordable per metre in this list, so for standard lighting and general-purpose circuits where FR is enough, it delivers unbeatable cost per metre from a reputable brand. It is not low-smoke, so pair it with an FR-LSH grade in enclosed areas.

items.name: APAR Shakti Green Wire (HR FR-LSH)
items.bestFor: Low-smoke safety on a tighter budget
items.body: APAR's Shakti Green Wire offers HR FR-LSH low-smoke insulation at a keener price than most premium lines. It is the sensible middle ground when you want better-than-FR fire behaviour without stepping all the way up to e-beam.

items.name: KEI Banfire (HFFR)
items.body: Banfire is halogen-free flame retardant (HFFR) with the best fire-emission numbers in the range: smoke density max 20% and acid-gas generation max 0.5%. When a fire must not produce toxic or corrosive fumes, in hospitals, hotels and crowded high-rises, this is the safety-first choice, at fair value for a specialist grade.

items.name: KEI Conflame (FR-LSH)
items.body: The standard Conflame is FR-LSH: flame retardant with low smoke and low halogen, oxygen index 29%+. A dependable step up from plain FR for everyday home wiring, well priced for the added safety.

items.name: Havells Life Line Plus S3 (HRFR)
items.body: Havells' Life Line Plus S3 is HRFR, rated 28A at 2.5 sq mm, with Havells' well-known quality control and low current-leakage insulation. It sits at the premium end on price per metre, so it is about brand trust and heat headroom rather than lowest cost, and still good value if brand is your deciding factor.

items.name: KEI HomeCab (FR)
items.body: HomeCab is KEI's FR line with anti-rodent and anti-termite insulation, lead-free, to IS 694. A solid, keenly discounted FR wire (around 46% off MRP on Elume) for standard circuits at project scale where low smoke is not required.

items.name: Finolex FR Wire
items.body: Finolex's FR house wire is a long-trusted staple for standard residential circuits: flame retardant, 1100V, IS 694, at an affordable per-metre price. A safe default when you want a heritage brand for general-purpose wiring.

buyingTips: Match gauge to load: use 1.0 sq mm for lighting points, 1.5 sq mm for general lighting and fan circuits, 2.5 sq mm for power sockets and 5A/15A points, 4 sq mm for geysers and small ACs, and 6 sq mm for high-load ACs and the main run. Undersizing causes heat and voltage drop; oversizing wastes money.

Insist on FRLS or HRFR for enclosed homes and commercial spaces. FR only slows flame spread, while FRLS adds low smoke and low halogen emission, which buys precious escape time and protects electronics in a fire. The small price premium is worth it.

Verify the BIS/ISI mark and IS 694 reference printed on the wire, along with conductor size, 1100V grade and brand. Check that a coil is genuinely 90 m by reading the sequential metre marking, and confirm the conductor is bright electrolytic-grade copper, not copper-clad aluminium.

Buy in project volume to unlock wholesale pricing. On Elume, ordering 15+ coils typically adds an extra 5% off the Elume price on top of the MRP discount, which adds up fast on a multi-floor wiring job. Standardise on one or two brands per site for easier inventory and colour coding.

faq.q: Which house wire gives the best value for money?
faq.a: Value for money means the most safety and quality per rupee, not the lowest price. Premium e-beam (EBXL) and HR FR-LSH grades like APAR Anushakti and Polycab Maxima+ Green Wire top our list because they deliver low-smoke, heat-resistant fire safety at a per-metre price close to standard FR wire, helped by deep discounts off MRP on Elume. For tight budgets on standard circuits, RR Kabel Superex FR is the lowest cost per metre from a trusted brand.

faq.q: What is the difference between FR and FRLS house wires?
faq.a: FR (Flame Retardant) insulation resists catching fire and slows flame spread. FRLS (Flame Retardant Low Smoke) does the same but also emits far less smoke and corrosive halogen gas when exposed to fire. For enclosed homes, apartments and commercial spaces, FRLS is the safer choice because reduced smoke gives occupants more time to escape.

faq.q: Which gauge wire should I use for an AC or geyser?
faq.a: For most household geysers and 1 to 1.5 ton split ACs, 4 sq mm copper wire is the standard, safe choice. Larger 2 ton or higher ACs and the main incoming run usually need 6 sq mm. Always size for the appliance's actual load and run length, and consult your electrician for long cable runs where voltage drop matters.

faq.q: Is copper or aluminium house wire better in India?
faq.a: For internal house wiring, copper is strongly preferred. It has higher conductivity, runs cooler, resists corrosion and flexes without fatigue, making it safer and more durable. All wires in this list use 99.97% electrolytic-grade copper. Aluminium is mainly used for service mains and large feeders, not for in-home lighting and socket circuits.

faq.q: How long is a standard house wire coil in India?
faq.a: The standard retail coil length for single-core house wire in India is 90 metres. Reputable brands print sequential metre marking along the wire so you can verify the full length. Always check this printing when buying, as short-length or unmarked coils are a common way buyers get short-changed on a project.

faq.q: How do I confirm a house wire is genuine and BIS-certified?
faq.a: Look for the ISI mark with the IS 694 standard number printed directly on the insulation, alongside the conductor size, 1100V voltage grade and brand name. Genuine wire has consistent, legible printing and bright copper strands inside. On Elume, all listed wires are BIS-certified to IS 694, so you buy with verified safety specs.
